title: hardening flags for fedora rpmbuilds
body:
This commit sets the CGO_CFLAGS variable for hardening the Fedora rpm
binaries.
    
The flags used are the same as used in the official Fedora rpms.
Setting the flags in upstream spec would provide early warnings for
flag adjustments or other hardening issues.


Signed-off-by: Lokesh Mandvekar <lsm5@fedoraproject.org>

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]".  That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.
-->


@baude @cevich @rhatdan PTAL.

comments.body:
LGTM
but definitely want someone with more spec background to review.

> LGTM
> but definitely want someone with more spec background to review.

yes, let's get @jnovy and @umohnani8 to look at this well.

I feel embarrassingly unqualified to review this. All I can do is confirm that the flags match [those in fedpkg master](https://src.fedoraproject.org/rpms/podman/blob/rawhide/f/podman.spec#_421-427), which they do apart from the `ix86` exception. I don't know if `ix86` is the right arch macro, nor do I know if `+=` will work in this environment (i.e. can we safely assume that the rpm build script will run under bash?), and don't even know how to verify or test. I have to assume that if either doesn't work, we'll find out.

With those caveats, LGTM.

@saschagrunert @vrothberg 
Does OpenSUSE use this spec file?  I would doubt they have the -specs=/usr/lib/rpm/redhat/redhat-hardened-cc1 file?

> I feel embarrassingly unqualified to review this. All I can do is confirm that the flags match [those in fedpkg master](https://src.fedoraproject.org/rpms/podman/blob/rawhide/f/podman.spec#_421-427), which they do apart from the `ix86` exception. I don't know if `ix86` is the right arch macro, nor do I know if `+=` will work in this environment (i.e. can we safely assume that the rpm build script will run under bash?), and don't even know how to verify or test. I have to assume that if either doesn't work, we'll find out.
> 
> With those caveats, LGTM.

Good point, I've yet to update rawhide branch. This was a copy-paste from the f33 branch where v3.0.0-rc3 koji build was failing earlier for i686. You can see the f33 change here: https://src.fedoraproject.org/rpms/podman/blob/f33/f/podman.spec#_463-472

The `+=` works for variable setting in spec file as in regular bash. We have used it in the fedora spec file for setting golang BUILDTAGS earlier.

Also:

```
$ rpm --eval %{ix86}
i386 i486 i586 i686 pentium3 pentium4 athlon geode
```

> @saschagrunert @vrothberg
> Does OpenSUSE use this spec file? I would doubt they have the -specs=/usr/lib/rpm/redhat/redhat-hardened-cc1 file?

*SUSE is using their own spec file.

> @saschagrunert @vrothberg
> Does OpenSUSE use this spec file? I would doubt they have the -specs=/usr/lib/rpm/redhat/redhat-hardened-cc1 file?

Ack, I'll put these under a fedora/centos conditional so it won't bother other distros.
adjusted the flag conditionals with comments explaining why.

LGTM. The comments are helpful, thank you.

If for any reason you need to resubmit, you may want to add the magic CI-colon-DOCS string to bypass unneeded CI.
